title: Inside Amazon's push to lower Alexa AI costs
excerpt: Internal documents show Amazon worked to lower Alexa+ AI costs by reducing the use of Anthropic models and getting more work from every GPU.
content_html:
Exclusive

How Amazon weaned Alexa off Anthropic's pricey models to slash AI costs

By Eugene Kim

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

Amazon has redesigned Alexa to rely less on Anthropic models, part of a sweeping effort to lower the cost of running its AI-powered voice assistant, according to internal documents reviewed by Business Insider.

The documents, which span late last year through early this year, show Amazon pursuing a series of changes in how Alexa generates answers by routing more requests to its in-house AI models, avoiding unnecessary calls to Anthropic's Claude models, and squeezing more work from each GPU.

Together, the initiatives were expected to more than quadruple the number of customer transactions each unit of computing capacity could support.

The effort offers a glimpse into AI's next battleground.

As frontier models become more capable, competition is shifting from building smarter AI to making them cheaper to run. Google has promoted lower-cost AI through Gemini Flash, while companies including OpenAI and Cursor have introduced techniques that automatically send simpler requests to lower-cost models.

Amazon's financial projections underscore why the company has devoted so much effort to this challenge.

Internal forecasts from early this year showed AWS cloud costs for the upgraded, AI-powered Alexa+ were on pace to reach roughly $1.7 billion in 2026, nearly triple the previous year.

Alexa+ was also projected to run about 60% above Amazon's target for AWS cloud cost per monthly active user. Even after identifying roughly $450 million in potential savings, internal reviews concluded the business would not hit its financial targets. Amazon declined to comment.

A costly new Alexa

Unlike earlier versions of Alexa, Alexa+ generates many responses with large language models running on GPU-intensive cloud services. That turned relatively inexpensive voice requests into AI workloads that cost far more to serve.

Those costs became more important as Amazon worked through a difficult launch. Business Insider previously reported that the company delayed Alexa+ multiple times as engineers grappled with AI hallucinations and questions about whether the service was ready for customers. Alexa+ expanded its availability in the US earlier this year.

Scaling the service only increased the financial pressure, a sign of how different generative AI is from more traditional software services.

As Alexa+ rolled out to more users, Amazon projected sharply higher AWS cloud spending as demand for AI computing capacity grew.

The company even weighed delaying some of its most expensive AI initiatives. Business Insider previously reported that Project Moonraker, Amazon's effort to give Alexa more advanced AI agent capabilities, was expected to become the service's largest AI expense this year, and the company considered delaying parts of the project as it searched for savings.

Reducing unnecessary calls to Claude

One of Amazon's priorities was narrowing where Anthropic's Claude models would be used inside Alexa+.

Internal roadmaps called for moving specialized Alexa "Experts" from Claude Sonnet to Amazon's own AI models while reducing other use of Claude across the digital-assistant service.

Amazon also sought to avoid inference whenever possible. Inference is how AI models are run, and one way to limit the cost of this is to use caching, which stores answers to common requests so the AI doesn't have to do the same work again.

One Amazon roadmap called for Alexa+ to stop calling Claude models when suitable answers were already available in cache, and expand "deterministic" handling, which enables Alexa to answer more predictable requests without tapping a large language model.

The strategy is notable given Amazon's deep ties to Anthropic. Amazon has invested billions in the AI startup, partners closely with it, and stands to reap a significant windfall from Anthropic's IPO, if that goes ahead.

Yet the official internal documents reviewed by Business Insider show Amazon has been looking for ways to reduce how often Alexa relies on Anthropic's models.

Amazon's approach mirrors a growing trend across the AI industry. Investment firm William Blair wrote in a recent report that software companies are starting to reserve frontier models for difficult, high-stakes reasoning while routing less complex requests to cheaper models. That lowers inference costs without changing the customer experience.

"Multi-model routing is becoming standard architecture in software," analysts at William Blair wrote in the report.

Delivering more with fewer GPUs

Reducing model costs was only one part of the strategy. Amazon also focused on increasing how much work each GPU could perform.

Rather than simply adding more Nvidia GPUs, Amazon wanted to process more customer requests from the same computing gear. One roadmap projected software upgrades would increase available computing capacity by roughly 50% while cutting response times by about 40%. Internal planning dashboards tracked projected customer growth, GPU utilization, available capacity and inference efficiency as Amazon prepared to scale Alexa+.

Amazon's cost-saving efforts extended beyond software. Planning documents show the company evaluating both Nvidia GPUs and its own Trainium chips to further lower the cost of running Alexa+.

More broadly, the documents show Amazon treating frontier AI models and GPU capacity as expensive resources to be deployed selectively rather than by default.

That philosophy echoes a point CEO Andy Jassy has made publicly. In his shareholder letter last year, Jassy argued there's an "urgency" to make AI inference dramatically less expensive.

"Reducing the cost per unit in AI will unleash AI being used as expansively as customers desire, and also lead to more overall AI spending," Jassy wrote.

Have a tip? Contact this reporter via email at ekim@businessinsider.com or Signal, Telegram, or WhatsApp at 650-942-3061. Use a personal email address, a nonwork WiFi network, and a nonwork device; here's our guide to sharing information securely.

Eugene is Business Insider’s Chief Tech Correspondent, where he leads coverage of Amazon. His reporting spans the company’s retail operations, AWS, Alexa, and its secretive internal work culture.Previously, he worked at CNBC, Fortune Magazine Korea, and Japan's Yomiuri Shimbun. He holds degrees from NYU and Columbia University’s Graduate School of Journalism.In 2022, Eugene broke a story uncovering Amazon’s practice of deceptively enrolling customers in Prime and deliberately making cancellation difficult. A year later, the Federal Trade Commission sued the company, citing his reporting. That case culminated in a record $2.5 billion settlement in 2025.His reporting has earned multiple honors, including the SF Press Club’s Bay Area Journalism Award and SPJ NorCal’s Excellence in Journalism Award.Eugene lives in the Bay Area. Contact him via email at ekim@businessinsider.com, or Signal, Telegram, or WhatsApp at 650-942-3061. 'We have been deliberately confusing,' former employee says.Inside Amazon's flailing brick-and-mortar ambitions: missed projections, pressure to cut costs, and a war with Whole FoodsInside Amazon's complex employee-review system, where workers feel left in the dark and managers expect to give 5% of reports bad reviewsAfter 28 years, 'Day 2' finally arrives at AmazonAWS, Alexa, healthcare:Inside Amazon's struggle to break into the lucrative market for SaaS business applications, including an internal pitch to buy $38 billion HubSpotInside Amazon's struggle to crack Nvidia's AI-chip dominanceAmazon's AI data center dream runs into the reality of 'zombie' facilities, higher costs, and labor shortagesAmazon is gutting its voice assistant, Alexa. Employees describe a division in crisis and huge losses on 'a wasted opportunity.'Amazon is working on a new 'Remarkable Alexa,' but internal politics and technical issues plague the projectAmazon projected huge losses from its healthcare business in 2024, but strong sales growth, internal document reveals

sentences.zh: 据《商业内幕》(Business Insider) 查阅的内部文件显示，亚马逊重新设计了 Alexa，减少对人类模型的依赖，这是降低其人工智能语音助手运行成本的全面努力的一部分。

blocks.html_en: The documents, which span late last year through early this year, show Amazon pursuing a series of changes in how Alexa generates answers by routing more requests to its in-house AI models, avoiding unnecessary calls to Anthropic's Claude models, and squeezing more work from each GPU.

sentences.zh: 这些文件从去年年底到今年年初，显示亚马逊正在对 Alexa 生成答案的方式进行一系列改变，将更多请求路由到其内部人工智能模型，避免对 Anthropic 的 Claude 模型进行不必要的调用，并从每个 GPU 中挤出更多工作。

blocks.html_en: Together, the initiatives were expected to more than quadruple the number of customer transactions each unit of computing capacity could support.

sentences.en: Together, the initiatives were expected to more than quadruple the number of customer transactions each unit of computing capacity could support.

blocks.html_en: The effort offers a glimpse into AI's next battleground.

sentences.en: The effort offers a glimpse into AI's next battleground.

blocks.html_en: As frontier models become more capable, competition is shifting from building smarter AI to making them cheaper to run. Google has promoted lower-cost AI through Gemini Flash, while companies including OpenAI and Cursor have introduced techniques that automatically send simpler requests to lower-cost models.

sentences.en: As frontier models become more capable, competition is shifting from building smarter AI to making them cheaper to run.
sentences.zh: 随着前沿模型的能力越来越强，竞争正在从构建更智能的人工智能转向降低运行成本。

sentences.en: Google has promoted lower-cost AI through Gemini Flash, while companies including OpenAI and Cursor have introduced techniques that automatically send simpler requests to lower-cost models.
sentences.zh: 谷歌通过 Gemini Flash 推广了低成本人工智能，而 OpenAI 和 Cursor 等公司也推出了自动向低成本模型发送更简单请求的技术。

blocks.html_en: Amazon's financial projections underscore why the company has devoted so much effort to this challenge.

sentences.en: Amazon's financial projections underscore why the company has devoted so much effort to this challenge.

blocks.html_en: Internal forecasts from early this year showed AWS cloud costs for the upgraded, AI-powered Alexa+ were on pace to reach roughly $1.7 billion in 2026, nearly triple the previous year.

sentences.en: Internal forecasts from early this year showed AWS cloud costs for the upgraded, AI-powered Alexa+ were on pace to reach roughly $1.7 billion in 2026, nearly triple the previous year.
sentences.zh: 今年年初的内部预测显示，到 2026 年，升级后的人工智能驱动的 Alexa+ 的 AWS 云成本将达到约 17 亿美元，几乎是前一年的三倍。

blocks.html_en: Alexa+ was also projected to run about 60% above Amazon's target for AWS cloud cost per monthly active user. Even after identifying roughly $450 million in potential savings, internal reviews concluded the business would not hit its financial targets. Amazon declined to comment.

sentences.en: Alexa+ was also projected to run about 60% above Amazon's target for AWS cloud cost per monthly active user.
sentences.zh: 预计 Alexa+ 的运行成本将比亚马逊每月活跃用户 AWS 云成本目标高出约 60%。

sentences.en: Even after identifying roughly $450 million in potential savings, internal reviews concluded the business would not hit its financial targets.
sentences.zh: 即使在确定了大约 4.5 亿美元的潜在节省之后，内部审查仍得出结论，该业务无法实现其财务目标。

sentences.en: Amazon declined to comment.
sentences.zh: 亚马逊拒绝置评。

sentences.en: Inference is how AI models are run, and one way to limit the cost of this is to use caching, which stores answers to common requests so the AI doesn't have to do the same work again.
sentences.zh: 推理是人工智能模型的运行方式，限制其成本的一种方法是使用缓存，它存储常见请求的答案，这样人工智能就不必再次执行相同的工作。

blocks.html_en: One Amazon roadmap called for Alexa+ to stop calling Claude models when suitable answers were already available in cache, and expand "deterministic" handling, which enables Alexa to answer more predictable requests without tapping a large language model.

sentences.en: One Amazon roadmap called for Alexa+ to stop calling Claude models when suitable answers were already available in cache, and expand "deterministic" handling, which enables Alexa to answer more predictable requests without tapping a large language model.
sentences.zh: 亚马逊的一份路线图要求 Alexa+ 在缓存中已有合适的答案时停止调用 Claude 模型，并扩展“确定性”处理，这使得 Alexa 能够在不利用大型语言模型的情况下回答更可预测的请求。

blocks.html_en: The strategy is notable given Amazon's deep ties to Anthropic. Amazon has invested billions in the AI startup, partners closely with it, and stands to reap a significant windfall from Anthropic's IPO, if that goes ahead.

sentences.en: Amazon has invested billions in the AI startup, partners closely with it, and stands to reap a significant windfall from Anthropic's IPO, if that goes ahead.
sentences.zh: 亚马逊已向这家人工智能初创公司投资了数十亿美元，并与其密切合作，如果 Anthropic 的 IPO 顺利进行，亚马逊将获得巨额意外之财。

blocks.html_en: Yet the official internal documents reviewed by Business Insider show Amazon has been looking for ways to reduce how often Alexa relies on Anthropic's models.

sentences.en: Yet the official internal documents reviewed by Business Insider show Amazon has been looking for ways to reduce how often Alexa relies on Anthropic's models.
sentences.zh: 然而，《商业内幕》(Business Insider) 查阅的官方内部文件显示，亚马逊一直在寻找方法来减少 Alexa 对 Anthropic 模型的依赖频率。

blocks.html_en: Amazon's approach mirrors a growing trend across the AI industry. Investment firm William Blair wrote in a recent report that software companies are starting to reserve frontier models for difficult, high-stakes reasoning while routing less complex requests to cheaper models. That lowers inference costs without changing the customer experience.

sentences.en: Amazon's approach mirrors a growing trend across the AI industry.
sentences.zh: 亚马逊的做法反映了整个人工智能行业的增长趋势。

sentences.en: Investment firm William Blair wrote in a recent report that software companies are starting to reserve frontier models for difficult, high-stakes reasoning while routing less complex requests to cheaper models.
sentences.zh: 投资公司 William Blair 在最近的一份报告中写道，软件公司开始保留前沿模型来进行困难、高风险的推理，同时将不太复杂的请求转移到更便宜的模型。

sentences.en: That lowers inference costs without changing the customer experience.
sentences.zh: 这可以在不改变客户体验的情况下降低推理成本。

blocks.html_en: "Multi-model routing is becoming standard architecture in software," analysts at William Blair wrote in the report.

sentences.en: "Multi-model routing is becoming standard architecture in software," analysts at William Blair wrote in the report.
sentences.zh: William Blair 的分析师在报告中写道：“多模型路由正在成为软件的标准架构。”

blocks.html_en: Delivering more with fewer GPUs

sentences.en: Delivering more with fewer GPUs
sentences.zh: 用更少的 GPU 提供更多服务

blocks.html_en: Reducing model costs was only one part of the strategy. Amazon also focused on increasing how much work each GPU could perform.

sentences.en: Reducing model costs was only one part of the strategy.
sentences.zh: 降低模型成本只是该战略的一部分。

sentences.en: Amazon also focused on increasing how much work each GPU could perform.
sentences.zh: 亚马逊还致力于增加每个 GPU 可以执行的工作量。

blocks.html_en: Rather than simply adding more Nvidia GPUs, Amazon wanted to process more customer requests from the same computing gear. One roadmap projected software upgrades would increase available computing capacity by roughly 50% while cutting response times by about 40%. Internal planning dashboards tracked projected customer growth, GPU utilization, available capacity and inference efficiency as Amazon prepared to scale Alexa+.

sentences.en: Rather than simply adding more Nvidia GPUs, Amazon wanted to process more customer requests from the same computing gear.
sentences.zh: 亚马逊不想简单地添加更多 Nvidia GPU，而是希望通过相同的计算设备处理更多的客户请求。

sentences.en: One roadmap projected software upgrades would increase available computing capacity by roughly 50% while cutting response times by about 40%.
sentences.zh: 一份路线图预计软件升级将使可用计算能力增加约 50%，同时将响应时间缩短约 40%。

sentences.en: Internal planning dashboards tracked projected customer growth, GPU utilization, available capacity and inference efficiency as Amazon prepared to scale Alexa+.
sentences.zh: 当亚马逊准备扩展 Alexa+ 时，内部规划仪表板跟踪预计的客户增长、GPU 利用率、可用容量和推理​​效率。

blocks.html_en: Amazon's cost-saving efforts extended beyond software. Planning documents show the company evaluating both Nvidia GPUs and its own Trainium chips to further lower the cost of running Alexa+.

sentences.en: Amazon's cost-saving efforts extended beyond software.
sentences.zh: 亚马逊节省成本的努力不仅限于软件。

sentences.en: Planning documents show the company evaluating both Nvidia GPUs and its own Trainium chips to further lower the cost of running Alexa+.
sentences.zh: 规划文件显示，该公司正在评估 Nvidia GPU 和自己的 Trainium 芯片，以进一步降低运行 Alexa+ 的成本。

blocks.html_en: More broadly, the documents show Amazon treating frontier AI models and GPU capacity as expensive resources to be deployed selectively rather than by default.

sentences.en: More broadly, the documents show Amazon treating frontier AI models and GPU capacity as expensive resources to be deployed selectively rather than by default.
sentences.zh: 更广泛地说，这些文件显示亚马逊将前沿人工智能模型和 GPU 能力视为昂贵的资源，需要有选择地部署，而不是默认部署。

blocks.html_en: That philosophy echoes a point CEO Andy Jassy has made publicly. In his shareholder letter last year, Jassy argued there's an "urgency" to make AI inference dramatically less expensive.

sentences.en: That philosophy echoes a point CEO Andy Jassy has made publicly.
sentences.zh: 这一理念呼应了首席执行官安迪·贾西 (Andy Jassy) 公开表达的观点。

sentences.en: In his shareholder letter last year, Jassy argued there's an "urgency" to make AI inference dramatically less expensive.
sentences.zh: 贾西在去年的股东信中表示，“迫切”需要大幅降低人工智能推理的成本。

blocks.html_en: "Reducing the cost per unit in AI will unleash AI being used as expansively as customers desire, and also lead to more overall AI spending," Jassy wrote.

sentences.en: "Reducing the cost per unit in AI will unleash AI being used as expansively as customers desire, and also lead to more overall AI spending," Jassy wrote.
sentences.zh: 贾西写道：“降低人工智能的单位成本将使人工智能能够按照客户的意愿得到广泛使用，同时也会带来更多的人工智能总体支出。”

blocks.html_en: Eugene is Business Insider’s Chief Tech Correspondent, where he leads coverage of Amazon. His reporting spans the company’s retail operations, AWS, Alexa, and its secretive internal work culture.Previously, he worked at CNBC, Fortune Magazine Korea, and Japan's Yomiuri Shimbun. He holds degrees from NYU and Columbia University’s Graduate School of Journalism.In 2022, Eugene broke a story uncovering Amazon’s practice of deceptively enrolling customers in Prime and deliberately making cancellation difficult. A year later, the Federal Trade Commission sued the company, citing his reporting. That case culminated in a record $2.5 billion settlement in 2025.His reporting has earned multiple honors, including the SF Press Club’s Bay Area Journalism Award and SPJ NorCal’s Excellence in Journalism Award.Eugene lives in the Bay Area. Contact him via email at ekim@businessinsider.com, or Signal, Telegram, or WhatsApp at 650-942-3061. Use a personal email address, a nonwork WiFi network, and a nonwork device; here's our guide to sharing information securely. ExpertiseAmazon, Jeff Bezos, Andy Jassy, e-commerce, and cloud computing.Popular ArticlesAmazon:Internal Amazon emails give an exclusive look at how CEO Andy Jassy has started to run the company, with obsessive attention to the retail business and what some employees feel is micromanagingAndy Jassy will be the next CEO of Amazon. Insiders dish on what it's like to work for Jeff Bezos' successor, who built AWS into a $40 billion business.Internal documents show Amazon has for years knowingly tricked people into signing up for Prime subscriptions. 'We have been deliberately confusing,' former employee says.Inside Amazon's flailing brick-and-mortar ambitions: missed projections, pressure to cut costs, and a war with Whole FoodsInside Amazon's complex employee-review system, where workers feel left in the dark and managers expect to give 5% of reports bad reviewsAfter 28 years, 'Day 2' finally arrives at AmazonAWS, Alexa, healthcare:Inside Amazon's struggle to break into the lucrative market for SaaS business applications, including an internal pitch to buy $38 billion HubSpotInside Amazon's struggle to crack Nvidia's AI-chip dominanceAmazon's AI data center dream runs into the reality of 'zombie' facilities, higher costs, and labor shortagesAmazon is gutting its voice assistant, Alexa. Employees describe a division in crisis and huge losses on 'a wasted opportunity.'Amazon is working on a new 'Remarkable Alexa,' but internal politics and technical issues plague the projectAmazon projected huge losses from its healthcare business in 2024, but strong sales growth, internal document reveals

sentences.zh: 他拥有纽约大学和哥伦比亚大学新闻研究生院的学位。 2022 年，尤金揭露了亚马逊欺骗性地为顾客注册 Prime 并故意让取消变得困难的做法。

sentences.en: A year later, the Federal Trade Commission sued the company, citing his reporting.
sentences.zh: 一年后，联邦贸易委员会援引他的报告起诉该公司。

sentences.en: That case culminated in a record $2.5 billion settlement in 2025.His reporting has earned multiple honors, including the SF Press Club’s Bay Area Journalism Award and SPJ NorCal’s Excellence in Journalism Award.Eugene lives in the Bay Area.
sentences.zh: 该案最终于 2025 年达成了创纪录的 25 亿美元和解金。他的报道赢得了多项荣誉，包括 SF 新闻俱乐部的湾区新闻奖和 SPJ NorCal 的卓越新闻奖。尤金住在湾区。

sentences.en: 'We have been deliberately confusing,' former employee says.Inside Amazon's flailing brick-and-mortar ambitions: missed projections, pressure to cut costs, and a war with Whole FoodsInside Amazon's complex employee-review system, where workers feel left in the dark and managers expect to give 5% of reports bad reviewsAfter 28 years, 'Day 2' finally arrives at AmazonAWS, Alexa, healthcare:Inside Amazon's struggle to break into the lucrative market for SaaS business applications, including an internal pitch to buy $38 billion HubSpotInside Amazon's struggle to crack Nvidia's AI-chip dominanceAmazon's AI data center dream runs into the reality of 'zombie' facilities, higher costs, and labor shortagesAmazon is gutting its voice assistant, Alexa.
sentences.zh: 前员工表示，“我们一直在故意混淆视听”。 亚马逊摇摇欲坠的实体野心：未能实现预期、削减成本的压力以及与全食超市的战争。 亚马逊复杂的员工审核系统内部，员工感觉被蒙在鼓里，经理们预计会给 5% 的报告差评。 28 年后，亚马逊终于迎来了“第二天”AWS、Alexa、医疗保健：亚马逊为打入利润丰厚的市场而奋斗的内部情况。 SaaS 商业应用程序，包括内部提议斥资 380 亿美元收购 HubSpot 亚马逊正在努力打破 Nvidia 的 AI 芯片主导地位，亚马逊的 AI 数据中心梦想遇到了“僵尸”设施、更高的成本和劳动力短缺的现实。亚马逊正在淘汰其语音助手 Alexa。

sentences.en: Employees describe a division in crisis and huge losses on 'a wasted opportunity.'Amazon is working on a new 'Remarkable Alexa,' but internal politics and technical issues plague the projectAmazon projected huge losses from its healthcare business in 2024, but strong sales growth, internal document reveals
sentences.zh: 员工们描述了陷入危机的部门以及“浪费机会”造成的巨大损失。亚马逊正在开发一款新的“卓越的 Alexa”，但内部政治和技术问题困扰着该项目内部文件显示，亚马逊预计 2024 年其医疗保健业务将遭受巨大损失，但销售增长强劲

content_html_zh:
独家的

亚马逊如何让 Alexa 摆脱 Anthropic 昂贵的模型以削减人工智能成本

作者：Eugene Kim 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

据《商业内幕》(Business Insider) 查阅的内部文件显示，亚马逊重新设计了 Alexa，减少对人类模型的依赖，这是降低其人工智能语音助手运行成本的全面努力的一部分。

这些文件从去年年底到今年年初，显示亚马逊正在对 Alexa 生成答案的方式进行一系列改变，将更多请求路由到其内部人工智能模型，避免对 Anthropic 的 Claude 模型进行不必要的调用，并从每个 GPU 中挤出更多工作。

这些举措预计将使每个计算能力单位可支持的客户交易数量增加四倍以上。

这项工作让我们得以一睹人工智能的下一个战场。

随着前沿模型的能力越来越强，竞争正在从构建更智能的人工智能转向降低运行成本。 谷歌通过 Gemini Flash 推广了低成本人工智能，而 OpenAI 和 Cursor 等公司也推出了自动向低成本模型发送更简单请求的技术。

亚马逊的财务预测强调了该公司为何投入如此多的努力来应对这一挑战。

今年年初的内部预测显示，到 2026 年，升级后的人工智能驱动的 Alexa+ 的 AWS 云成本将达到约 17 亿美元，几乎是前一年的三倍。

预计 Alexa+ 的运行成本将比亚马逊每月活跃用户 AWS 云成本目标高出约 60%。 即使在确定了大约 4.5 亿美元的潜在节省之后，内部审查仍得出结论，该业务无法实现其财务目标。 亚马逊拒绝置评。

昂贵的新 Alexa

与 Alexa 的早期版本不同，Alexa+ 通过在 GPU 密集型云服务上运行的大型语言模型生成许多响应。 这将相对便宜的语音请求变成了服务成本更高的人工智能工作负载。

随着亚马逊经历了艰难的推出，这些成本变得更加重要。 《商业内幕》此前报道称，由于工程师们努力应对人工智能的幻觉以及该服务是否已为客户做好准备的问题，该公司多次推迟了 Alexa+。 Alexa+ 今年早些时候扩大了在美国的可用性。

扩展服务只会增加财务压力，这表明生成式人工智能与更传统的软件服务有多么不同。

随着 Alexa+ 向更多用户推出，随着人工智能计算能力需求的增长，亚马逊预计 AWS 云支出将大幅增加。

该公司甚至考虑推迟一些最昂贵的人工智能计划。 Business Insider 此前曾报道称，亚马逊为 Alexa 提供更先进的人工智能代理功能的 Project Moonraker 预计将成为该服务今年最大的人工智能支出，该公司考虑推迟部分项目，以节省成本。

减少对克劳德不必要的通话

亚马逊的首要任务之一是缩小 Anthropic 的 Claude 模型在 Alexa+ 中的使用范围。

内部路线图呼吁将专门的 Alexa“专家”从 Claude Sonnet 转移到亚马逊自己的人工智能模型，同时减少 Claude 在数字助理服务中的其他使用。

亚马逊还尽可能避免推断。 推理是人工智能模型的运行方式，限制其成本的一种方法是使用缓存，它存储常见请求的答案，这样人工智能就不必再次执行相同的工作。

亚马逊的一份路线图要求 Alexa+ 在缓存中已有合适的答案时停止调用 Claude 模型，并扩展“确定性”处理，这使得 Alexa 能够在不利用大型语言模型的情况下回答更可预测的请求。

鉴于亚马逊与 Anthropic 的深厚联系，这一战略值得注意。 亚马逊已向这家人工智能初创公司投资了数十亿美元，并与其密切合作，如果 Anthropic 的 IPO 顺利进行，亚马逊将获得巨额意外之财。

然而，《商业内幕》(Business Insider) 查阅的官方内部文件显示，亚马逊一直在寻找方法来减少 Alexa 对 Anthropic 模型的依赖频率。

亚马逊的做法反映了整个人工智能行业的增长趋势。 投资公司 William Blair 在最近的一份报告中写道，软件公司开始保留前沿模型来进行困难、高风险的推理，同时将不太复杂的请求转移到更便宜的模型。 这可以在不改变客户体验的情况下降低推理成本。

William Blair 的分析师在报告中写道：“多模型路由正在成为软件的标准架构。”

用更少的 GPU 提供更多服务

降低模型成本只是该战略的一部分。 亚马逊还致力于增加每个 GPU 可以执行的工作量。

亚马逊不想简单地添加更多 Nvidia GPU，而是希望通过相同的计算设备处理更多的客户请求。 一份路线图预计软件升级将使可用计算能力增加约 50%，同时将响应时间缩短约 40%。 当亚马逊准备扩展 Alexa+ 时，内部规划仪表板跟踪预计的客户增长、GPU 利用率、可用容量和推理​​效率。

亚马逊节省成本的努力不仅限于软件。 规划文件显示，该公司正在评估 Nvidia GPU 和自己的 Trainium 芯片，以进一步降低运行 Alexa+ 的成本。

更广泛地说，这些文件显示亚马逊将前沿人工智能模型和 GPU 能力视为昂贵的资源，需要有选择地部署，而不是默认部署。

这一理念呼应了首席执行官安迪·贾西 (Andy Jassy) 公开表达的观点。 贾西在去年的股东信中表示，“迫切”需要大幅降低人工智能推理的成本。

贾西写道：“降低人工智能的单位成本将使人工智能能够按照客户的意愿得到广泛使用，同时也会带来更多的人工智能总体支出。”

有小费吗？ 请通过电子邮件联系本记者：ekim@businessinsider.com 或 Signal、Telegram 或 WhatsApp：650-942-3061。 使用个人电子邮件地址、非工作 WiFi 网络和非工作设备；这是我们安全共享信息的指南。

尤金是《商业内幕》的首席技术记者，负责亚马逊的报道。 他的报道涵盖公司的零售业务、AWS、Alexa 及其秘密的内部工作文化。此前，他曾在 CNBC、韩国《财富》杂志和日本读卖新闻工作。 他拥有纽约大学和哥伦比亚大学新闻研究生院的学位。 2022 年，尤金揭露了亚马逊欺骗性地为顾客注册 Prime 并故意让取消变得困难的做法。 一年后，联邦贸易委员会援引他的报告起诉该公司。 该案最终于 2025 年达成了创纪录的 25 亿美元和解金。他的报道赢得了多项荣誉，包括 SF 新闻俱乐部的湾区新闻奖和 SPJ NorCal 的卓越新闻奖。尤金住在湾区。 通过电子邮件联系他：ekim@businessinsider.com，或者拨打 Signal、Telegram 或 WhatsApp：650-942-3061。 使用个人电子邮件地址、非工作 WiFi 网络和非工作设备；这是我们安全共享信息的指南。 专业知识亚马逊、杰夫·贝索斯、安迪·贾西、电子商务和云计算。 热门文章亚马逊：亚马逊内部电子邮件独家报道了首席执行官安迪·贾西如何开始运营公司，对零售业务的过度关注以及一些员工认为微观管理的安迪·贾西将成为亚马逊的下一任首席执行官。 内部文件显示，亚马逊多年来一直在故意欺骗人们注册 Prime 订阅。 前员工表示，“我们一直在故意混淆视听”。 亚马逊摇摇欲坠的实体野心：未能实现预期、削减成本的压力以及与全食超市的战争。 亚马逊复杂的员工审核系统内部，员工感觉被蒙在鼓里，经理们预计会给 5% 的报告差评。 28 年后，亚马逊终于迎来了“第二天”AWS、Alexa、医疗保健：亚马逊为打入利润丰厚的市场而奋斗的内部情况。 SaaS 商业应用程序，包括内部提议斥资 380 亿美元收购 HubSpot 亚马逊正在努力打破 Nvidia 的 AI 芯片主导地位，亚马逊的 AI 数据中心梦想遇到了“僵尸”设施、更高的成本和劳动力短缺的现实。亚马逊正在淘汰其语音助手 Alexa。 员工们描述了陷入危机的部门以及“浪费机会”造成的巨大损失。亚马逊正在开发一款新的“卓越的 Alexa”，但内部政治和技术问题困扰着该项目内部文件显示，亚马逊预计 2024 年其医疗保健业务将遭受巨大损失，但销售增长强劲
